subject: Simple quota unsafe? RIP: 0010:__btrfs_free_extent.isra.0+0xc41/0x1020 [btrfs] / do_free_extent_accounting:2999: errno=-2 No such entry

reviews.author: Marc MERLIN
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the system was experiencing crashes due to btrfs quota issues, and provided details about their specific setup, including nightly snapshots, subvolume deletion, and hourly/daily/weekly snapshots. They mentioned that they have quotas still running on a single host but turned off balance and scrub jobs as a workaround.
reviews.raw_body:
All 3 filesystems this happened on were btrfs receive recipients, so
that created snapshots nightly (when it crashed each time).
Also after btrfs receive, my script deletes the oldest backup that rolls
out the rotation.
Then I also have some amount btrfs-snaps that makes hourly/daily/weekly
snapshots

So yes, multiple per day per subvolume multipled by many subvolume
(10-ish)

I have squotas still running on a single host but I turned off btrfs
balance nightly cronjob as well as btrfs scrub, and it has not crashed
yet (it's a 20TB+ backup recipient built from scratch, I woudl love for
squotas to work on it)

Marc
-- 
"A mouse is a device used to point at the xterm you want to type in" - A.S.R.
 
Home page: http://marc.merlins.org/                       | PGP 7F55D5F27AAF9D08

2026-04-16.patch_summary: This patch is a response to an issue where the btrfs file system is experiencing errors when trying to free extents, resulting in a '-2 No such entry' error. The developer, Boris Burkov, is seeking more information about how the quota was being managed before the error occurred, specifically whether subvolumes or qgroups were manually deleted.
